prerequisites: A Vantage account with access to the cost and usage data you want to extract.
An API access token created in the Vantage console. The token must have at least Read scope to retrieve workspaces, cost reports, and related data.

authMethods.name: Bearer access token
authMethods.summary: Vantage authenticates API requests with a bearer token. The connector sends the token in the access_token field, which is passed as the HTTP header 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'. Vantage supports two token types you can create in the console: Service Tokens (account-level) and Personal Access Tokens (user-level); either works as the access_token value. Tokens are issued with Read and/or Write scope.

setupSteps.title: Create an API access token in Vantage
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Vantage and go to Settings, then API Access Tokens. To create a user-level token, open the Personal Access Tokens tab, enter a name, and choose a scope. To create an account-level token, use the Service Tokens (API Access Tokens) view, enter a name, select a team if you are on Enterprise, and choose a scope. Since this is a read connector, Read scope is sufficient. Click Create, then click Show to reveal and copy the token value.

setupSteps.title: Provide the access token to the connector
setupSteps.body: Set the access_token config field to the token you copied from Vantage. The connector authenticates against the Vantage API base URL https://api.vantage.sh/v2/ by sending the header 'Authorization: Bearer <access_token>' on each request.

setupSteps.title: Verify the credentials
setupSteps.body: Confirm the token works by issuing an authenticated request, for example GET https://api.vantage.sh/v2/workspaces with the 'Authorization: Bearer <access_token>' header. A successful response confirms the token is valid and has the required scope before running a sync.
